title: Why Hasan (RA) Chose Peace Instead of War | Sh Ammar Alshukry
description:
Why Hasan (RA) Chose Peace Instead of War

Many people think choosing peace means giving up.
But Hasan ibn Ali (RA), the grandson of the Prophet ﷺ, showed that true strength is knowing when peace serves the Ummah better than conflict.

Sometimes the strongest leader isn't the one who fights.
It's the one who prevents bloodshed.

What do you think was the greatest lesson from Hasan's (RA) decision?

Watch the full lecture on our YouTube channel

#Islam #Muslim #HasanIbnAli #Leadership #Reminder #AlMaghrib

title: How Shaytan Actually Attacks You | Sh. Saad Tasleem | Al Maghrib Podcast
description:
What if you could study Shaytan the way a therapist studies a patient, by analyzing his own words in the Quran? 

Sh. Saad Tasleem joins Sh. Ammar AlShukry to talk about his new book Shaytan, a comprehensive study built from more than ten years of teaching.

They get into the psychology of Shaytan, why we were given so few physical descriptions of him, how his attacks are tailored to your specific fears and desires, and the step by step method he uses to move a person from a small slip to something far worse. Along the way there are stories worth hearing, including a thobe shop in Medina that revealed how Shaytan really operates, and honest talk about fame, ego, and why every one of us needs people who will tell us the truth.

Sh. Saad teaches Deception with AlMaghrib and his new book 'Shaytan' can be found at AlMaghrib events in your city: https://www.almaghrib.org

Chapters
0:00 Welcome and the all black book
1:04 The Viking vest and when to give fashion advice
4:15 Why he waited until 40 to publish
6:08 Losing his parents and finishing the book
6:47 Why a book on Shaytan
8:29 Writing for scholars and everyday readers
11:01 The Medina thobe shop and the psychology of Shaytan
22:39 Studying Shaytan's own words
24:54 Firaun, arrogance, and delusions of grandeur
28:03 What does Shaytan actually look like
31:40 Why we shouldn't reduce him to horror movies
35:01 Israiliyat and sticking to authentic sources
36:33 His animosity toward humanity
39:30 Favorite traps, waswasa, and Khinzab
42:23 The step by step method and how good people fall
45:25 When a sin is private and when to get help
46:52 Why everyone needs honest advisors
49:23 The fitna of fame
52:11 You can't wing it: the Yassir Birjas story
57:12 The most helpful takeaways
59:00 Taking Shaytan as an enemy
1:00:45 The biggest takeaway: the isti'adah
1:04:29 Where to get the book

#Shaytan #SaadTasleem #AlMaghribPodcast #Deception #Islam

title: A Poem About Beauty and the Divine | Sh Ammar Alshukry
description:
Have you ever seen a dog stunned by a sunset, or a bird taken by the sea below it? Sh. Ammar AlShukry shares a poem he wrote about beauty and what our love of it says about us. If we were only animals like the rest, why are our souls so easily captured by beautiful places, beautiful moments, and beautiful people? 

Maybe the One who made beauty also made us, and our love of it points us back to Him.

From Sh. Ammar AlShukry's latest podcast with Sh. Fahad Tasleem. Watch the full episode here: https://youtu.be/UHF52R8Ggt0

#Poetry #Beauty #Iman #AmmarAlShukry #AlMaghribPodcast

title: When Peace Is Greater Than Victory | Sh. Ammar Alshukry
description:
There are moments in history that changed the future of the Ummah forever.

One of those moments came when Hasan ibn Ali (RA), the beloved grandson of the Prophet ﷺ, made the difficult decision to pursue reconciliation over continued conflict. His choice united the Muslims and fulfilled a prophecy of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ.

In this powerful reminder, Sh. Ammar Alshukry explores the remarkable leadership of Hasan ibn Ali (RA), the principles of reconciliation in Islam, and timeless lessons on justice, sincerity, and true strength that remain just as relevant today.

What does it really mean to be a peacemaker?

Watch until the end and share your biggest takeaway in the comments.

Learn more about IlmSummit:
https://www.almaghrib.org/ilmsummit/

Subscribe for more beneficial reminders and Islamic lectures from AlMaghrib.

#Islam #HasanIbnAli #Leadership #Muslim #Reminder #AlMaghrib

title: The Most Underrated Person in the Masjid
description:
Who is the most underrated person in the masjid?

Most of us think about the imam.

But what about the one who arrives before everyone else, opens the masjid, prepares everything, and calls the believers to prayer?

In this clip, Shaykh Abu Eesa reflects on the often-overlooked role of the mu'adhin and why we should have greater appreciation for those who serve our communities behind the scenes.

Sometimes the greatest acts of worship are the ones few people notice.

Discover the wisdom and beauty behind salah in Divine Link: The A-Z of the Fiqh of Salah.

title: Can You Move During Salah?
description:
Your phone starts ringing in the middle of salah.

Do you ignore it because you're praying? Or can you move?

Many Muslims think any movement invalidates the prayer. But the fiqh of salah is far more balanced than that.

In this clip, Shaykh Abu Eesa explains when movement is not only permissible, but sometimes even necessary, whether it's silencing your phone or closing a gap in the row.

Learning the fiqh of salah isn't about making prayer harder. It's about knowing how to worship Allah with confidence in real-life situations.

Discover the practical rulings of prayer in Divine Link: The A-Z of the Fiqh of Salah.

title: How Should Muslims Respond to AI?
description:
Should Muslims reject AI?

Not at all.

The real question is whether we can benefit from technology without allowing it to shape our worldview.

This is a conversation every Muslim should be having as AI becomes part of our everyday lives.

💭 How do you think Muslims should approach AI?
🎙️ Watch the full AlMaghrib Podcast episode on YouTube.

#ArtificialIntelligence #Islam #Muslims #Technology #Quran

title: Should You Pray When You Need the Bathroom?
description:
You're about to pray...

But you're starving.
Or you really need to use the bathroom.

Should you pray anyway?

In this clip, Shaykh Abu Eesa explains an important distinction: your prayer may still be valid, but will you be able to give Allah the focus and presence that salah deserves?

Prayer isn't something to rush through or "get over with." It's the most important appointment of your day.

Learn the fiqh and wisdom behind a truly meaningful salah in Divine Link: The A-Z of the Fiqh of Salah.

title: AI Won't Challenge Your Faith the Way You Think
description:
Most people think AI will challenge our beliefs through arguments.

Sh. Fahad Tasleem explains why the greater challenge may be something far more subtle: our experiences.

If technology begins shaping how we experience reality, how do we stay grounded in the truth Allah revealed?

💭 What are your thoughts?

🎙️ Watch the full AlMaghrib Podcast episode on YouTube.

#ArtificialIntelligence #Islam #Muslims #Faith #Technology

title: Was Taraweeh Really Started by the Prophet ﷺ?
description:
Most Muslims assume Taraweeh was established exactly as we know it today by the Prophet ﷺ.

The reality is more nuanced.

In this clip, Shaykh Abu Eesa explains the relationship between Qiyam al-Layl, Tahajjud, and Taraweeh, and how this beautiful practice developed during the earliest generations of Islam.

Studying fiqh isn't just about learning rulings. It's about understanding the history, wisdom, and evidence behind them.

Explore the fiqh of salah with confidence in Divine Link: The A-Z of the Fiqh of Salah.

title: What Happens When Aliens Claim They Created Us | Sh Fahad Tasleem
description:
Sh. Fahad Tasleem lays out why this matters. The deception does not arrive all at once. It moves step by step. First people are asked to accept the idea of extraterrestrials, which is theologically possible for us. Then comes the disclosure that says these beings are here, and by the way, they created you. At that point you are forced to backtrack and scramble for a response. His point is to think ahead to the moment aliens are claimed to be our creators, so we are ready if it ever happens. This is not something every Muslim needs to dive into, but leaders of the community should at least be familiar with it.

Watch the full episode on the AlMaghrib Podcast

title: You Missed Fajr. Don't Make This Mistake.
description:
You overslept.

Fajr has already ended.

Now what?

For many people, that's where Shaytan begins his work. He whispers that it's too late, that you've already failed, and that there's no point trying anymore.

In this powerful reminder, Shaykh Abu Eesa exposes one of Shaytan's greatest tricks: turning one mistake into complete hopelessness.

Islam never teaches us to despair of Allah's mercy. Every prayer is another opportunity to return to Him.

Learn the fiqh, wisdom, and beauty of salah in Divine Link: The A-Z of the Fiqh of Salah.

title: Remembering Srebrenica
description:
This weekend marks the 31st anniversary of one of the darkest events for the Ummah and humanity in recent history: The massacre of Srebrenica.

During the Bosnian war in the 90s, as part of a genocidal campaign against the Bosnian Muslims, Serbian forces gathered men and boys from the town of Srebrenica, systematically murdering them, whilst brutalising the women and girls of the town.

It’s reported that over 8000 men and boys were brutally killed in the ethnic cleansing, with many bodies still being uncovered to this day, three decades later, with an annual collective janazah for the newly identified victims.

It was a crime reflecting the ugly and enduring legacy of Islamophobia, one that we still see around us today.

Our own Shaykh Yaser Birjas, who spent time doing aid work in the aftermath of the war, took a group of our students to visit the memorial site at Srebrenica, sharing some important reflections on remembering the martyrs of Bosnia.

May Allah have mercy on the Shuhada of yesterday and today, and protect our brothers and sisters facing oppression across the globe.

title: Schoolchildren Who All Drew the Same Alien | Sh Fahad Tasleem
description:
Sh. Fahad Tasleem points to John Mack, a well known Harvard psychiatrist who spent years studying alien abduction accounts as a psychological and psychiatric phenomenon. He was ridiculed for it, but the cases are well documented, and people describe the same thing across different parts of the world. The classic image of the gray, with the big eyes and the small body, keeps showing up. In one documented case in Zimbabwe, schoolchildren said they watched a craft land and interacted with these beings. Asked to draw what they saw, they nearly all drew the same figure. Interviewed years later as adults, they still stand by their story. They were children at the time, and they had no reason to lie.

Watch the full episode on the AlMaghrib Podcast!
